semanticCore: Youth, clarity, ruling, judgment, or advice
quranicRelevance: Young men/youths (Fityah/Fata - 'innahum fityatun amanoo'), servant/companion, and seeking a ruling (Ifta' - 'yastaftoonaka').

keyDerivations.meaning: Young man, youth, page, or servant
To give a formal ruling/opinion, or ask for a ruling

figurativeOrRareUsages: إِنَّهُمْ فِتْيَةٌ آمَنُوا بِرَبِّهِمْ (18:13): Indeed they were youths who believed in their Lord (representing vigor and pure conviction).
أَفْتُونِي فِي أَمْرِي (27:32): Advise/give me your opinion in my affair (Queen of Sheba).
discardedNoise: The historical evolution of Islamic legal institutions (Mufti).
summaryEn: The root links youthful vigor (Fata) with intellectual/legal clarity (Fatwa), covering youths of faith, servants, and legal consultations.
summaryAr: يربط هذا الجذر الفتوة والبيان، واصفاً طاقة وحيوية الشباب المؤمن (فتية) والإفتاء والبيان القانوني أو الشوري (أفتوني).
rawText: فتى 1 , (T, O, K,) [aor. ﹹ,] inf. n. فَثٌّ, (T,) He scattered the dates of his جُلَّة [or receptacle made of palm-leaves]. (T, O, * K.) === And فَثَّ المَآءَ الحَارَّ بِالبَارِدِ, aor. ﹹ, (M, TA,) inf. n. فَثٌّ, (TA,) He abated, or allayed, the heat of the hot water by means of the cold: from Yaakoob. (M, TA.) [See also فَثَأَ.]
